steps.title: Estimate the slope from the data trend
steps.content:
The scatterplot stretches from a low cluster near $(1, 2)$ up to the highest dot near $(5, 15)$.

Approximate slope using the endpoints:
$$\text{slope} \approx \frac{15 - 2}{5 - 1} = \frac{13}{4} \approx 3.3$$
So the line of best fit must have slope close to 3.3, not a fraction less than 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the slope = 0.82 choices
steps.content: Choices A and B both have slope $0.82$. Over the data range of 4 units in $x$, that would only raise $y$ by $0.82 \times 4 \approx 3.3$ units — far too shallow for the $\sim\!13$-unit rise visible in the plot. Eliminate A and B.

steps.title: Test C and D at the data center
steps.content:
The data cluster is roughly centered near $(x, y) = (3, 11)$. Plug $x = 3$ into the remaining choices:
  • Choice C — $y = 3.30x + 0.82$: $y = 9.90 + 0.82 = 10.72$. Very close to $11$. ✓
  • Choice D — $y = 3.30x - 3.30$: $y = 9.90 - 3.30 = 6.60$. Far below the cluster. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
